# Code of Conduct

Welcome to the Magos community — we’re glad you’re here! Whether you’re
contributing code, asking questions, or sharing ideas, you’re helping shape a
space where collaboration and curiosity thrive.

To help everyone feel respected and included, we’ve put together some simple
guidelines that apply across all community spaces — including GitHub, Discord,
and beyond.

This space is dedicated to focused, technical work related to the development
of Magos. It’s everyone’s responsibility to help maintain a respectful
environment, especially when disagreements occur. We encourage you to be
kind, constructive, and open-minded in your interactions with others.

By participating, you agree to adhere to the [Magos Community Guidelines](https://magosproject.io/community-guidelines).
We ask everyone to treat each other with empathy, kindness, and respect. That
means being open to different perspectives, choosing your words thoughtfully,
and helping create a space where everyone can contribute and learn without
fear or exclusion.

Thanks for being part of Magos. Let’s build something great together.
